description: Fix OpenTelemetry dependency version misalignment in packages/observability that causes typecheck and build failures, then remove the inapplicable Brakeman workflow, and update the autonomous status file.
steps: Step 1: Update packages/observability/package.json to align all OpenTelemetry deps to the v2/0.218.x generation. Specifically: keep @opentelemetry/api at ^1.9.0 (API package stays on v1.x). Update @opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node from ^1.30.0 to ^2.7.0. Update @opentelemetry/sdk-metrics from ^1.30.0 to ^2.7.0. Update @opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http from ^0.57.0 to ^0.218.0. Update @opentelemetry/exporter-metrics-otlp-http from ^0.57.0 to ^0.218.0. Update @opentelemetry/resources from ^1.30.0 to ^2.7.0. Keep @opentelemetry/semantic-conventions at ^1.28.0. Update @opentelemetry/instrumentation-http from ^0.57.0 to ^0.218.0. Update @opentelemetry/instrumentation-fastify from ^0.43.0 to ^0.218.0. Keep @opentelemetry/sdk-node at ^0.218.0.
Step 2: Run `pnpm install` (NOT --frozen-lockfile) to regenerate the lockfile with the new dependency versions.

Step 3: Check if packages/observability/src/sdk/tracing.ts and packages/observability/src/sdk/metrics.ts still compile. The v2 Resource API may have changed - in v2, the Resource constructor may need a different import or factory function. If `new Resource({...})` no longer works, look at the installed @opentelemetry/resources package to see what API is exported (check node_modules/@opentelemetry/resources/build/src/Resource.d.ts or similar). The fix may be to use `resourceFromAttributes()` or a different constructor pattern. Also verify ATTR_SERVICE_NAME is still exported from @opentelemetry/semantic-conventions.
Step 4: Run `pnpm typecheck --filter @quant/observability` to confirm the observability package compiles cleanly.
Step 5: Run `pnpm typecheck` (full monorepo) to verify no other packages broke.
Step 6: Run `pnpm turbo test` to verify all tests pass.
Step 7: Run `pnpm turbo build --concurrency=3` to verify the full build passes.
Step 8: Remove .github/workflows/brakeman.yml - this is a Ruby security scanner running on a pure TypeScript/Node.js monorepo. It generates false positives and is not applicable.

Step 9: Update .agents/state/quant-autonomous-status.json to reflect the verified state of all gates. Set last_updated to current timestamp. Update gate_notes with the fix details. Set all passing gates to 'pass'. Remove any stale notes about OTel errors. Add a note about the brakeman workflow removal.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm typecheck passes for all packages (0 errors)
pnpm turbo test passes (all tests green)
pnpm turbo build --concurrency=3 passes (all packages build successfully)
.agents/state/quant-autonomous-status.json is updated with accurate gate status
